LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WEBCOR/OBAYASHI JOINT VENTURE'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS OF THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION OF SKANSKAS COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT WEBCOR/OBAYASHI JOINT VENTURE'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR REFORMATION, AND MODERN PRACTICE ALLOWS A PARTY TO PLEAD IN THE ALTERNATIVE AND MAKE INCONSISTENT ALLEGATIONS. (MENDOZA V. CONTINENTAL SALES CO. (2006) 140 CAL.APP.4TH 1395, 1402.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR	711, CKAREN@SFTC.ORG
MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR JAN-02-2018 VACATED. DISMISSAL OF COMPLAINT FILED ON 10/12/17. (206)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Fourth Cause Of Action Of Skanskas Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Aaron Gruber, Esq. (650-445-0543) of Ralls Gruber & Niece LLP for Plaintiff.
Rebecca Takacs, Esq. (510-903-2006) of OLes Morrison for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT WEBCOR/OBAYASHI JOINT VENTURE'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings of the Fourth Cause Of Action Of Skanskas Complaint. Defendant Webcor/Obayashi Joint Venture's motion for judgment on the pleadings is DENIED. The complaint sufficiently pleads a cause of action for reformation, and modern practice allows a party to plead in the alternative and make inconsistent allegations. (Mendoza v. Continental Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/rbu)

Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers And/Or Further Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Aaron Gruber, P.E., of Ralls Gruber & Niece LLP, Tel. 650.445.0543, for plaintiffs;
Rebecca D. Takacs, of Oles Morrison Rinker Baker LLP, Tel. 510.903.2001, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PLAINTIFF SKANSKA USA BUILDING, INC., Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers And/Or Further Discovery Responses is granted. Request For Sanctions is denied. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
